summary: Finished splitting the monolithic asm/sdata.s small-data file into per-module .sdata sections, removed asm/sdata.s from the build, and deleted the remaining asm/sysdolphin/baselib/list.s assembly file. The PR also promoted the baselib random seed symbols from anonymous sdata labels to C globals named seed and seed_ptr, updated sda21 references to seed_ptr, and replaced several C references to sdata string labels with local/static strings, string literals, or __FILE__.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sdata.s, a 919-line monolithic small read/write data file containing .section .sdata entries for many unrelated baselib and system symbols.
Removed SDATA_O_FILES from the Makefile O_FILES list and deleted the asm/sdata.o entry from obj_files.mk, so the build no longer links a standalone sdata object.

Added .section .sdata blocks directly to many owning assembly files under asm/sysdolphin/baselib, including AXDriver.s, bytecode.s, class.s, displayfunc.s, fog.s, initialize.s, jobj.s, pobj.s, robj.s, synth.s, texp.s, wobj.s, and others.
Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/list.s, indicating the list module no longer needed its separate assembly file in this slice.
Changed src/sysdolphin/baselib/random.c from extern anonymous labels lbl_804D5F90 and lbl_804D5F94 to real C definitions: u32 seed = 1; and u32* seed_ptr = &seed.
Updated random-number code and inline assembly references from lbl_804D5F94 to seed_ptr, including HSD_Rand, HSD_Rand_inline, HSD_Randf assembly comments/operands, and _HSD_RandForgetMemory.
Updated asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s to load seed_ptr@sda21(r13) instead of lbl_804D5F94@sda21(r13).
Updated include/variables.h comments to refer to seed_ptr rather than lbl_804D5F94.
Changed src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.c to stop using extern sdata string labels for assertions and instead use __FILE__, "list", and "prev".
Changed src/sysdolphin/baselib/object.c to pass the literal "hsd_obj" to hsdInitClassInfo instead of an extern sdata string label.
Adjusted small static string definitions in aobj.c, dobj.c, and id.c to include explicit trailing NUL characters and sizes, such as "aobj.c\0", "dobj.c\0", and "entry\0".
smart_moves: Moved small-data entries into the same assembly files as the code that logically owns them, reducing the global dumping-ground effect of asm/sdata.s and making future decompilation attribution easier.
Removed the build-system references to SDATA_O_FILES at the same time as deleting asm/sdata.s, avoiding a stale object-file dependency.
Promoted the random seed state to named C globals, seed and seed_ptr, while preserving the sda21 access pattern required by existing assembly references.
Used source-level constructs where possible, such as __FILE__ and string literals in list.c/object.c, instead of keeping unnecessary extern references to anonymous sdata labels.
Preserved explicit layout details for split .sdata blocks, including .balign 4 and NULL padding, which is important for matching small-data address order.
Updated cross-module assembly references such as gmmain.s when renaming lbl_804D5F94 to seed_ptr, preventing symbol drift between C and assembly.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a monolithic .sdata file, migrate entries into the owning object files and update the object list so the linker order still matches without a separate sdata.o.
Small-data symbols can sometimes be converted from anonymous lbl_ names to meaningful C globals if all sda21 references are updated consistently.
String labels used only for assertions or class names may be replaced with __FILE__ or string literals when that preserves emitted data and ordering.
Explicit string sizes and explicit "\0" text can be useful matching levers for forcing the compiler to emit the same small-data layout as hand-written assembly.
Do not assume every sdata item should become C immediately; this PR leaves many labels as lbl_804Dxxxx in per-module assembly when evidence for better names is not present.
Large data-section-only cleanups can still matter for decomp progress because they reduce global assembly dependencies and make later source-file matching less entangled.

naming_conventions.context: Baselib random seed value in src/sysdolphin/baselib/random.c.
Pointer to random seed, referenced through sda21 in random.c assembly and asm/melee/gm/gmmain.s.

naming_conventions.pattern: Retain lbl_804Dxxxx names for split .sdata items when no semantic name is evidenced.
naming_conventions.context: Most migrated .sdata entries in baselib assembly files kept address-style labels.

naming_conventions.pattern: Prefer source literals or __FILE__ over extern small-data string labels when matching allows it.
naming_conventions.context: list.c and object.c replaced labels such as lbl_804D5F48, lbl_804D5F50, lbl_804D5F60, and lbl_804D5F68.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Append .section .sdata blocks to the relevant asm/sysdolphin/baselib/*.s files, commonly before existing .sbss sections, to preserve object-local data ownership.
Maintain .balign 4 after .asciz strings and keep explicit .4byte NULL padding where present in the original monolithic sdata layout.
For sda21 small-data references, rename the symbol operand consistently across assembly files, e.g. lbl_804D5F94@sda21(r13) to seed_ptr@sda21(r13).
Use explicit char array sizes and explicit trailing NULs in C static strings when the compiler's default string object size would not match the expected data layout.
Remove obsolete assembly object entries from obj_files.mk and Makefile after moving their data into other objects, rather than leaving an empty or stale section file.

review_feedback.text: The PR context contains no issue comments, review comments, or reviews, so no human review feedback is evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert remaining lbl_804Dxxxx .sdata labels in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/*.s into C globals or literals.
Search for additional uses of seed_ptr and seed to confirm all random-state references were fully renamed after this PR.
Compare post-PR linker map order for .sdata to verify that per-object .sdata splitting preserved addresses after removing asm/sdata.o.
Look for subsequent removal or decompilation of other standalone baselib assembly files after list.s was deleted.
